name: the-spy-and-the-traitor
description: Ben Macintyre's gripping account of Oleg Gordievsky, the KGB colonel who became one of MI6's most valuable double agents, and Aldrich Ames, the CIA mole who betrayed him — the greatest espionage story of the Cold War.
useCase: Understanding Cold War espionage, KGB tradecraft, double-agent psychology, the betrayal that almost destroyed Western intelligence, and the most daring exfiltration in intelligence history.
knownRisks: The narrative is one-sided (British intelligence perspective)
Some details remain classified and are reconstructed from interviews
The book has been criticized for portraying Gordievsky in an overly heroic light
Some historical context (Soviet perspective) is minimized
